elements.sent-2411-002: An edge-coloring of a graph $G$ assigns a color to each edge of $G$.
elements.sent-2411-003: An edge-coloring is a parity edge-coloring if for each path $P$ in $G$, it uses some color on an odd number of edges in $P$.
elements.sent-2411-004: It is a strong parity edge-coloring if for every open walk $W$ in $G$, it uses some color an odd number of times along $W$.
elements.sent-2411-005: The minimum numbers of colors in parity and strong parity edge-colorings of $G$ are denoted $p(G)$ and $\spec(G)$, respectively.
elements.sent-2411-006: We characterize strong parity edge-colorings and use this characterization to prove lower bounds on $\spec(G)$ and answer several questions of Bunde, Milans, West, and Wu.
elements.sent-2411-008: We study special edge-colorings of graphs, where an edge-coloring of a graph assigns a color to each edge.
elements.sent-2411-009: Given an edge-coloring, a path $P$ is a parity path if each color appears an even number of times on $P$.
elements.sent-2411-010: Similarly, a walk $W$ is a parity walk if each color appears an even number of times along a traversal of $W$.
elements.sent-2411-011: A parity edge-coloring (pec) of a graph $G$ is an edge-coloring that admits no parity path.
elements.sent-2411-012: Similarly, a strong parity edge-coloring (spec) of $G$ is an edge-coloring that admits no open parity walk (a walk is open when its endpoints are distinct; otherwise it is closed).
elements.sent-2411-013: The parity edge chromatic number, written $p(G)$, is the minimum number of colors in a parity edge-coloring of $G$, and the strong parity edge chromatic number, written $\spec(G)$, is the minimum number of colors in a strong parity edge-coloring of $G$.
